title: rubus × semiangustifactus
intro: Rubus × semiangustifactus, also known as semi-erect thimbleberry, is a hybrid plant that belongs to the rose family and is a cross between the thimbleberry (Rubus parviflorus) and the black raspberry (Rubus occidentalis). It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its high antioxidant content and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to protect against cell damage and reduce inflammation in the body. The health benefits of Rubus × semiangustifactus include its ability to support cardiovascular health, improve cognitive function, and provide antimicrobial and antiviral protection. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal and health-promoting properties.
